lore.title: Description
lore.text: So little is known of the deep crows that even less is known of their monstrous leviathan cousins, the ancient deep crows. Whether these gargantuan specimens are elder deep crows grown to great size or some higher form that holds lesser deep crows in thrall remains to be determined. Ideally by someone else. Seriously, stay away from these things.

special_abilities.title: Magic Resistance
special_abilities.description: The ancient deep crow has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

special_abilities.title: Shadow Stealth
special_abilities.description: While in dim light or darkness, the ancient deep crow can take the Hide action as a bonus action.

special_abilities.title: Multiattack
special_abilities.description: The ancient deep crow makes three attacks: one with its mandibles and two with its claws.

actions.title: Mandibles
actions.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (2d10 + 6) piercing damage, and the target is grappled (escape DC 19). Until this grapple ends, the target is restrained, and the ancient deep crow can’t use its mandibles on another target.

sequential_actions.title: restrained

actions.title: Claw
actions.text: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d6 + 6) slashing damage.

actions.title: Shadow Caw
actions.text: The ancient deep crow releases an ear-splitting caw. Each creature within 60 feet of the crow and able to hear it must make a DC 17 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, a creature takes 10 (3d6) psychic damage.

legendary_action.text: The ancient deep crow can take 3 legendary actions, choosing from the options below. Only one legendary action option can be used at a time and only at the end of another creature’s turn. The ancient deep crow regains spent legendary actions at the start of its turn.

lair_actions.title: Detect
lair_actions.text: The deep crow makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

lair_actions.title: Shadow Caw (Costs 2 Actions)
lair_actions.text: The ancient deep crow uses Shadow Caw.

lair_actions.title: Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions)
lair_actions.text: The ancient deep crow beats its wings. Each creature within 10 feet of the deep crow must succeed on a DC 19 Dexterity saving throw or take 13 (2d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. The ancient deep crow can then fly up to half its flying speed.

lair_action.title: A Deep Crow’s Lair
lair_action.text: Deep crows and ancient deep crows roost in places both deep and warm, favoring sites with access to exposed lava. The spires of volcanic rock the creatures prize as roosts are common in such places, whose ambient warmth provides constant temperature regulation for the creature’s eggs.

regional_effect.title: Description
regional_effect.text: The region containing an ancient deep crow’s lair is transformed by the creature’s presence, which creates one or more of the following effects

region_effects.title: Supernatural shadow
region_effects.text: Supernatural shadow turns all bright light to dim light in underground regions within 6 miles of the lair.

region_effects.title: Echoing caws
region_effects.text: Intermittent, echoing caws can be heard coming from all directions within 6 miles of the lair.

region_effects.title: Subterranean beasts
region_effects.text: Subterranean beasts within 1 mile of the ancient deep crow’s lair serve as the creature’s eyes and ears, alerting it to the presence of intruders and making it all but impossible to surprise the ancient deep crow.
